differentials.name: Trapping defect =  defective cellular uptake of iodine into thyroid, salivary glands, gastric mucosa ◊  High doses of inorganic iodine facilitate diffusion into thyroid permitting a normal rate of thyroid hormone synthesis ◊  Normal ratio of iodine concentration for gastric juice÷plasma = 20÷1
differentials.raw: Trapping defect =  defective cellular uptake of iodine into thyroid, salivary glands, gastric mucosa ◊  High doses of inorganic iodine facilitate diffusion into thyroid permitting a normal rate of thyroid hormone synthesis ◊  Normal ratio of iodine concentration for gastric juice÷plasma = 20÷1

differentials.name: Organification defect =  deficient peroxidase activity, which catalyzes the oxidation of iodide by H2O2 to form monoiodotyrosine
differentials.raw: Organification defect =  deficient peroxidase activity, which catalyzes the oxidation of iodide by H2O2 to form monoiodotyrosine (MIT) / diiodotyrosine (DIT)

differentials.name: Deiodinase (dehalogenase) defect =  deficient deiodination of MIT / DIT to release iodide, which is reutilized to synthesize thyroid hormone production
differentials.raw: Deiodinase (dehalogenase) defect =  deficient deiodination of MIT / DIT to release iodide, which is reutilized to synthesize thyroid hormone production

differentials.name: identification of MIT + DIT in serum + urine following administration of 131I
differentials.raw: identification of MIT + DIT in serum + urine following administration of 131I

differentials.name: elevated free T4, elevated free T3, or both
differentials.raw: elevated free T4, elevated free T3, or both

differentials.name: Marine-Lenhart syndrome = nodular Graves = Graves disease coexistent in multinodular goiter
differentials.raw: Marine-Lenhart syndrome = nodular Graves = Graves disease coexistent in multinodular goiter

differentials.name: tachycardia, weight loss, muscle weakness, anxiety, decreased temperature tolerance
differentials.raw: tachycardia, weight loss, muscle weakness, anxiety, decreased temperature tolerance

context: √  nearly entire dose of administered radioiodine is excreted within 24 hours √  high thyroidal uptake of radioiodine / pertechnetate √  rapid 131I turnover √  positive perchlorate washout test Pendred syndrome = autosomal recessive trait of deficient peroxidase regeneration characterized by hypothyroidism + goiter + nerve deafness √  high thyroidal 131I uptake √  rapid intrathyroidal turnover of 131I √  goiter √  stippled epiphyses Thyrotoxicosis =  clinical syndrome of increased systemic metabolism Cause: (a)  increased thyroid function Hyperthyroidism =  excessive thyroid hormone activity R
